entries.goal: Idle clicker, mobile portrait. Center: a giant tappable target (cookie/coin emoji or simple SVG circle), tapping it +1 to score. Below: an 'Auto-clickers' shop where score can be spent — Cursor (10 score = +1/sec), Grandma (100 = +5/sec), Factory (1000 = +20/sec). Score updates every 100ms from auto-clickers. Persist score + owned counts to localStorage. Haptic on each tap. Touch-only, portrait, no keyboard.
entries.notes: Casual idle game. L1 — simplest possible mobile-game pattern. Validates the mobile wisdom pack at the L1 fast-path tier (single API call). If this can't ship clean, the wisdom rules need tuning.

entries.user_outcome: PUBLISHED 2026-04-25 — works fine. (Status said failed in some surface but artifact was good — separate UX bug worth investigating; flagged.)
entries.goal: Tip calculator, mobile-first. Single-screen portrait. Top: large bill amount input (number pad opens on tap). Middle: row of tip-percent quick-pick chips (10/15/18/20/custom). Bottom: split-by stepper (1-10 people). All inputs flow into 3 large-format result rows: tip total, grand total, per-person amount. Each result row has a tap-to-copy button. Use 100dvh + safe-area insets so the keyboard doesn't push results off-screen. Touch-first.
entries.notes: Mobile-first utility (not a game). Tests viewport sizing under iOS keyboard (mob-003), thumb-reach result placement (mob-004), tap-to-copy (mob-005). Variant of catalog's existing tip-calc but with explicit mobile constraints — direct comparison candidate to see if 'mobile-first' steering changes the artifact.

entries.user_outcome: PUBLISHED 2026-04-25 — solid, user shipped. Mobile-first variant validated against desktop pomodoro — direct A/B win. (harness timeout@300s false-positive)
entries.goal: Pomodoro timer, mobile-first portrait. Top: massive countdown (font-size clamp 48-96px) showing MM:SS. Below: a single huge primary button (Start/Pause/Resume) — sized 80% screen-width so the user's thumb can't miss it. Below that: a smaller 'Skip' button. 25min work / 5min break cycle, automatic phase transition with strong visual + audio chime. Cycle counter at top. Persist current phase + remaining seconds to localStorage so a tab-switch doesn't lose progress (mob-012). Phase color changes (work=red, break=green). Touch-only.
entries.notes: Mobile-first productivity. Tests full mobile pack: viewport, thumb-reach, haptic+audio (audio gesture-unlock per mob-009), state-persist across tab-switch (mob-012). Variant of saturated 'pomodoro' theme but justified by mobile-specific constraints. Direct comparison candidate vs. desktop pomodoro generations.

entries.goal: Make me a mobile color-strobe / disco screen tool. Full-screen tappable area cycles through saturated colors. TAP to advance one color (Red→Orange→Yellow→Green→Cyan→Blue→Magenta→White → loop). LONG-PRESS to start auto-cycle (configurable speed via slider 0.2s-2s per color). DOUBLE-TAP to lock the current color (full-brightness solid). Brightness slider via opacity overlay. Portrait full-bleed, no chrome. Apply mobile-frontend wisdom rules.
entries.notes: Replaces flashlight (no Wake Lock — relies on browser keeping screen on while user interacts) — pure CSS color strobe.

entries.goal: Build a mobile zen-circle drawing trainer tool. Big white canvas, draw a single closed circle with one continuous swipe (pointerdown → pointermove → pointerup). On release, score the roundness (variance from best-fit circle radius, 0-100 score). Show the user's stroke vs an overlaid 'perfect' circle. Best score persists. Portrait full-bleed. Apply mobile-frontend wisdom rules.
entries.notes: Zen-circle ratings via least-squares circle fit. Continuous-pointer drawing test.

entries.goal: Coin flip web mini-app. Big circular coin in center showing HEADS or TAILS. TAP to flip — coin spins via CSS-3D rotateY transform for 800ms then settles on a random face. Tap & hold = 'extra wobble' (longer animation). Counter at top: H wins / T wins, persisted. Confetti burst on every flip. Portrait full-bleed. Apply mobile-frontend wisdom rules.
entries.notes: Pure-CSS-3D flip animation. No DeviceMotion — tap is the only input.

entries.goal: Make me a mobile reaction-time game. Big START button. After random 1-3s delay, screen flashes green and a tap-target appears at random location — measure ms from flash to pointerdown. Show last + best reaction time. 'False start' if user taps during the wait period. Portrait, full-bleed. Apply mobile-frontend wisdom rules.
entries.notes: Tests precise input timing via performance.now() — common reaction-test mechanic.

entries.goal: Mobile stopwatch with lap times. Single big screen: huge MM:SS.mm readout (clamp font scaling). Two thumb-reachable buttons at the bottom: Start/Stop (left, primary action) + Lap/Reset (right). Lap button records current time to a scrollable list above the controls. Persist last 50 laps to localStorage. Portrait, full-bleed. Apply mobile-frontend wisdom rules.
entries.notes: Standard stopwatch but emphasizes thumb-reach + clamp-scaled font + lap persistence.

entries.goal: Fruit Ninja-lite. Fruits (apple/orange/banana/watermelon emoji on circular sprites) toss up from the bottom in groups of 1-4 at random arcs. Drag-swipe across the screen to slice them — each slice plays a satisfying particle-burst (split halves fly apart with physics). Bombs occasionally toss up — slicing them ends the game. Combo multiplier for slicing 3+ fruits in one continuous swipe. 60s timer. Portrait, full-bleed. Persist best score. Apply mobile-frontend wisdom rules.
entries.notes: Tests continuous-pointermove drag with setPointerCapture, projectile physics, slice-detection geometry.

entries.goal: Doodle Jump-lite web. Character auto-bounces up between procedurally-generated platforms scrolling top-to-bottom. DRAG horizontally on the bottom 30% of the screen to steer (relative offset, not absolute) — finger movement = horizontal velocity. Screen wraps horizontally (off left edge → reappears right). Game over if you fall off the bottom. Distance-meter at top. Portrait full-bleed. Apply mobile-frontend wisdom rules.
entries.notes: Bottom-drag steering replaces tilt. Wrap-around screen geometry + procedural platforms.

entries.goal: Piano Tiles-lite. 4 vertical lanes, black tiles scroll downward at increasing speed, tap each tile when it reaches the bottom-strike-zone (44px-tall band). Missing or tapping a white tile = game over. WebAudio: each tile plays a short piano note from a C-major pentatonic. Combo + score top-of-screen. Tile speed accelerates every 10 taps. Persist best score. Portrait full-bleed. Apply mobile-frontend wisdom rules.
entries.notes: Rhythm tap-game with strict 44px touch targets (mob-002) + WebAudio per-tile note + ramping difficulty.

entries.goal: 2048 mobile-touch. 4x4 grid, swipe in 4 directions to slide+merge tiles. SWIPE direction = pointerdown→pointerup vector (no on-screen buttons). Animate tile slide + merge with CSS transitions. Show next-best-tile preview top-right. Persist best score + current board to localStorage. Portrait, full-bleed (bottom-edge insets respected). Apply mobile-frontend wisdom rules. NOTE: 2048 IS in the saturated list but mobile-swipe-only variant is novel.
entries.notes: Pure-swipe 2048, no button chrome. Tests pointer-vector → direction inference + persistent state across reloads.

entries.goal: Helix Jump-lite. Vertical helix tower with rotating colored ring-platforms (some have gaps). Ball auto-falls — DRAG horizontally to rotate the entire tower and align the gap with the ball's column. Ball passes through gap = +1 level + camera pans down. Ball lands on a non-gap = bounce, OR on the same color twice = game over. 8+ levels with varying gap counts. Visual flash on level-clear. Portrait, full-bleed. Persist best level. Apply mobile-frontend wisdom rules.
entries.notes: 3D-feel via 2D rotation. Drag-to-rotate-world-not-character is a novel mobile gesture.

entries.goal: Mobile quick-note. Big bottom-thumb-zone textarea (autofocus, 60% screen height, 18px font). Above it: 6 most-recent notes as horizontally scrolling chip cards (200px wide, swipe to delete with red action sheet). Tap a chip to load it back into the textarea. Auto-save on every keystroke (debounced 300ms) to localStorage. Long-press a chip = pin to top. Search bar at very top (filters chips live). Portrait, full-bleed (with safe-area insets). Apply mobile-frontend wisdom rules.
entries.notes: Tests bottom-thumb-zone primary-input ergonomics + horizontal-scroll chip pattern + long-press contextual menu.

entries.goal: Mobile grocery list with swipe-to-complete. Big add-input at the bottom (autofocus on item add). List items scroll above. SWIPE-LEFT on an item to mark it bought (slide-out animation, item moves to a collapsed 'bought' section at the bottom). SWIPE-RIGHT to delete (fade + remove). Tap item to edit inline. Categorize via emoji prefix (auto-suggest 🥦 for veggies, 🥩 for meat, 🥛 for dairy from a small built-in dictionary). Persist to localStorage. Pull-down-to-add-from-recent-history (last 50 unique items as quick-tap chips). Portrait, full-bleed. Apply mobile-frontend wisdom rules.
entries.notes: Swipe-left vs swipe-right disambiguation + emoji auto-categorize + pull-down-to-show-recents.

entries.goal: Mobile finger-paint canvas. Full-screen canvas (100dvh, background white). Drag finger to paint with smooth bezier strokes (pointer interpolation between samples for smoothness at high velocities). Bottom strip: 8 color swatches (44px each, mob-002), brush-size slider (2-30px), undo button, clear button, save-to-photo button (uses canvas.toBlob → download .png). Pinch-to-zoom on canvas (two-finger pointers). Long-press a color = open hex-input modal. Portrait, full-bleed. Apply mobile-frontend wisdom rules.
entries.notes: Tests multi-pointer (pinch-zoom), bezier path smoothing, and toBlob download. Stretch: undo stack.

entries.goal: Stack-the-tower mobile game. A horizontal block slides back and forth across the screen at the top of the current tower. TAP to drop it — it lands on the existing top block. Any portion that hangs off the edge gets sliced off (the next block is narrower). Stack as high as you can before a block becomes too narrow to land. Camera pans up as tower grows. Color palette shifts hue per block. Visual flash on perfect stack (within 5px alignment = bonus + tower DOESN'T narrow). Portrait, full-bleed. Persist best height. Apply mobile-frontend wisdom rules.
entries.notes: Single-tap timing-game with perfect-stack reward mechanic + camera scrolling. Hue-shift palette is delightful detail.

entries.goal: Color Switch-lite. A small ball auto-falls due to gravity. TAP anywhere to make the ball jump up (impulse). Below: a vertical sequence of rotating obstacles (rings, crosses, squares, half-circles), each split into 4 colored segments. The ball is always one of those 4 colors — only passes through obstacle segments matching its color. Color-switcher pickups change the ball's color. Camera pans up as you climb. Game over if ball falls off bottom. Portrait full-bleed. Persist best height. Apply mobile-frontend wisdom rules.
entries.notes: Color-aware collision detection + rotating multi-color obstacles + 1-tap input. Major challenge: clean rotating-obstacle hit-testing.

entries.goal: Temple Run-lite endless runner. Pseudo-3D corridor (perspective-skewed lanes, NOT actual WebGL — use scaled 2D layers). 3 lanes. Character auto-runs forward. SWIPE LEFT / RIGHT to change lane. SWIPE UP = jump (clears low obstacles). SWIPE DOWN = slide (clears high obstacles). Coins to collect, obstacles to dodge, distance counter, increasing speed. Game over on collision. Visual flash on coin grab + crash. Persist best distance + total coins. Portrait full-bleed. Apply mobile-frontend wisdom rules.
entries.notes: 4-direction swipe disambiguation + pseudo-3D rendering + endless procedural-spawn pattern. Hard L3.

entries.goal: Crossy Road-lite. Top-down chunky pixel character. SWIPE in 4 directions to hop ONE GRID CELL at a time. Cross alternating lanes: roads with cars (don't get hit), rivers with logs (must land on a log or you drown), grass (safe). Camera auto-scrolls upward — if you fall behind off the bottom edge, game over. Procedurally spawn lanes ahead. Score = highest row reached. Portrait full-bleed. Visual flash on death + every 25 rows. Persist best. Apply mobile-frontend wisdom rules.
entries.notes: Discrete grid + 4-way swipe-hop + multi-rule lane types (cars/logs/safe) + auto-scroll death gate. L3 because of procedural lane gen + collision logic + log-riding edge cases.
entries.user_outcome_note: 2026-04-25 user feedback: "Hopper was pretty good!" Shipped as Hop Quest, 37KB, 5/9 mobile-pack tokens.

entries.goal: Frogger mobile. Frog at bottom, must cross 5 lanes of traffic + a river with logs to reach the top safely. SWIPE to hop one cell in any direction. River requires landing on a log (rides with it). Off the screen with the log = drown. Frog spawns are placed at the top safe-zone (5 home slots — fill all 5 = bonus level). Timer per crossing. Portrait full-bleed. Visual flash on death. Persist best level + total frogs home. Apply mobile-frontend wisdom rules.
entries.notes: Classic Frogger but mobile-touch + with home-slot fill mechanic. Tests 4-way swipe + log-rider physics + per-level timer.

entries.goal: Bejeweled-lite mobile match-3 (different from Candy Crush — emphasis on chains + power gems). 8x8 grid of gems (6 shapes). DRAG-swap two adjacent gems to make a 3+ match. 4-match → makes a flame gem (clears entire row/column on next match). 5-match → makes a hyper-cube (tap to clear all of one color). Cascades fall + auto-match. Score-based level progression (every 1500 pts = level up = faster spawn). Persist best score + level. Portrait full-bleed. Apply mobile-frontend wisdom rules.
entries.notes: Match-3 with power-gem creation + cascades. Different from CandyCrush (which was levels-based) — this is endless-mode classic Bejeweled.

entries.goal: Mobile music pad — 4x4 grid of colored beat pads. TAP each pad to toggle a step in a 16-step sequencer (current row). 4 rows = 4 instruments (kick, snare, hat, perc). BPM slider at top (60-180). Play/Stop button + clear-row button. Pads light up as the playhead crosses them. WebAudio: programmatic drum sounds (simple oscillators + noise + envelopes). Save 4 pattern slots to localStorage. Portrait full-bleed. Apply mobile-frontend wisdom rules.
entries.notes: 16-step grid sequencer + WebAudio synth. Tests mob-009 (resume AudioContext on first tap) + sample-accurate scheduling. Major LLM challenge: clean envelope shapes + low-CPU-overhead playback.

entries.goal: Mobile anagram word game. 6 letter tiles in a horizontal strip — DRAG-rearrange them to form a valid English word (3-6 letters). Tap-and-hold a letter to lock it in place. SHUFFLE button (bottom-right thumb zone) randomizes letter order. New 6-letter set every 60s; goal is to find as many words as possible. Bundled JSON of common English words (~3000). Persist best score. Portrait full-bleed. Apply mobile-frontend wisdom rules.
entries.notes: Drag-rearrange + tap-and-hold-to-lock + shuffle button (replacing shake). Pure pointer events.

entries.goal: Mobile Hangman with on-screen keyboard. Hidden word at top (under blanks). Custom 7×4 letter-keyboard at the bottom (in thumb-reach, 44px+ targets). Tap a letter — if in word, fills blanks; if not, draws next limb. 6 wrong guesses = game over. Categories: animals, foods, places, sports (chosen at start via 4-button modal). Persist win-streak + best-streak. Portrait full-bleed. Apply mobile-frontend wisdom rules. NOTE: hangman IS in saturated list but mobile-touch keyboard variant + category-pick is novel.
entries.notes: Novel mobile UX for classic hangman — keyboard ergonomics is the bar.

entries.goal: Letter Blast — mobile word puzzle. 5x5 grid of letter tiles. Tap-and-DRAG a path through adjacent tiles (orthogonal+diagonal) to spell a word ≥3 letters. Path lights up as you drag; release to commit. Used letters refill from above with gravity. 90-second round, score = sum of (length²) per word. Bonus: 5+ letter words trigger 'BLAST' that clears 3-tile radius. Persist best round score. Portrait full-bleed. Apply mobile-frontend wisdom rules.
entries.notes: Boggle/Wordscapes hybrid — drag-path word selection + gravity refill + radius-blast bonus.

entries.goal: Mobile rhyme race. Show 1 source word at top (e.g. 'CAT'). Below, 6 word-button options — tap any that rhyme. 60s round, +1 point per correct, -1 per wrong. New source word every 4s. Categories: 'easy 3-letter', 'medium 4-5 letter', 'hard 5+'. Use a small bundled rhyme-lookup JSON (curated 200 source words → rhyme arrays). Visual flash on correct. Portrait full-bleed. Apply mobile-frontend wisdom rules.
entries.notes: Quick-fire rhyming for kids/wordplay. Tests bundled-data lookup + tight per-tap timing.

entries.goal: Mobile mini-crossword (5x5 daily). Standard crossword grid with black squares + numbered clues. Tap a cell to start typing — virtual keyboard slides up from bottom (custom, full-width, 44px+ keys). Auto-advance to next cell, auto-jump on word complete. Across/Down clue list scrollable above keyboard, current clue highlighted. Cell-by-cell auto-check (green=correct, red=wrong) toggleable. Persist current puzzle state + completion stats. Portrait full-bleed. Apply mobile-frontend wisdom rules.
entries.notes: Crossword UX is a classic-mobile-design challenge: virtual keyboard + clue/grid layout in 375px-wide portrait. Single bundled puzzle as L3 demo.

entries.goal: Klondike Solitaire mobile. 7-tableau-column layout fits portrait via columns 1-7 in two rows (or compact horizontal scroll). Cards are small (45x65px) but tap-target ≥44px via overlay. DRAG card-or-card-stack from one column to another. Auto-flip top of column on uncover. Foundation piles top-right. Stock + waste top-left. Tap stock to deal 3. Persist current game + best-time + win-count. Portrait full-bleed. Apply mobile-frontend wisdom rules. NOTE: solitaire IS in saturated list but cards-on-portrait-mobile is non-trivial layout challenge.
entries.notes: Hard layout problem: 7 tableau columns at ~52px/card-width + drag-stacks. Tests setPointerCapture + portrait constraint creativity.

entries.goal: Blackjack mobile. Dealer hand top, player hand bottom. 4 huge thumb-zone buttons: HIT, STAND, DOUBLE, SPLIT (44+ px each, bottom 25% per mob-004). Card sliding-deal animation. Bet sizing: tap chip-stack to add ($5/$25/$100). Bankroll persists. Animations on win (golden flash + vibrate). Portrait full-bleed. Apply mobile-frontend wisdom rules. NOTE: blackjack IS saturated; this variant emphasizes mobile-touch ergonomics + bankroll-persistence.
entries.notes: Tests thumb-zone primary-action layout + bet-chip-stack interaction + bankroll-survival. mob-004 / mob-005 / mob-009.

entries.goal: Uno mobile (vs 3 AI). Bottom of screen: your 7-card fan, scrollable horizontally. Center: discard pile + draw deck. Top: 3 AI opponents with card-counts. Tap a card to play (if legal — match color/number/wild); if illegal, vibrate + flash red. SWIPE-up on a card to play it; SWIPE-down to draw. AI auto-plays in 1s intervals. Wild-card → modal with 4 color buttons. Persist win-rate. Portrait full-bleed. Apply mobile-frontend wisdom rules.
entries.notes: Multi-card hand UI on portrait + swipe-play vs swipe-draw + simple AI. Hard L3 challenge: card legality engine + AI heuristics.

entries.goal: Memory match-pairs mobile. 4×6 card grid (24 cards = 12 pairs). TAP card to flip. Match 2 = stays face-up; mismatch = both flip back after 1s. Timer + move-counter. Themes: emojis (animals/foods/sports/transport — pick at start). Visual flash on match. Confetti + win-time on full-clear. Persist best-time-per-theme. Portrait full-bleed. Apply mobile-frontend wisdom rules. NOTE: memory IS saturated; novel mobile addition is theme-pick + per-theme leaderboard.
entries.notes: 4×6 grid in portrait must be tested — 80px cards. Mobile-pack should reinforce thumb-target sizing.

entries.goal: Mobile mini-deckbuilder roguelike. 1-card-per-turn combat: bottom hand has 5 cards drawn from your deck (15 cards total, expandable). TAP to play; cards have cost (energy 0-3) + effect (attack/block/heal). Enemy at top has HP/intent indicator. After each fight, choose 1 of 3 reward cards to add to your deck. 5-fight run, then boss. Mid-run save. Portrait full-bleed. Apply mobile-frontend wisdom rules.
entries.notes: Slay-the-Spire-mini. Tests turn-based state machine + persistent deck + simple enemy AI + portrait card-row layout.

entries.goal: Tic-Tac-Toe with twist: 4×4 'connect-4-in-a-line' instead of 3×3 (more interesting, fits portrait). Two-player local: tap to place X/O. Or vs-AI mode (minimax depth-3). Big cells (≥80px). Win-line animates with golden trail. Visual flash on win. Persist W-L-D record + per-mode stats. Portrait full-bleed. Apply mobile-frontend wisdom rules.
entries.notes: Connect-4-in-line variant (4x4 grid, line=4) is significantly more strategic than 3x3 + works on portrait. Minimax AI as L2 stretch.

entries.goal: Dots and Boxes mobile. 6×6 dot grid. TAP between two adjacent dots to draw a line. When you complete the 4th side of a square, you claim it (filled with your color) and get another turn. Vs-AI greedy strategy. Score = boxes claimed. Visual flash on box-claim. Portrait full-bleed. Persist W-L-D. Apply mobile-frontend wisdom rules.
entries.notes: Pencil-and-paper classic. Tap between dots is a tight hit-zone challenge — 44px overlay around each potential edge.

entries.goal: Mobile Checkers (American Checkers / Draughts). 8×8 board. TAP your piece to select; valid-move squares highlight green. TAP a green square to move. Captures auto-chained (multi-jump). King promotion on opponent's back row. Vs-AI alpha-beta depth-4. Portrait: board fills width, controls + score below. Persist record + best-streak. Apply mobile-frontend wisdom rules.
entries.notes: Tap-select-then-tap-target is the standard board-game UX. Hard L3 because of valid-move highlighting + multi-jump chain + alpha-beta AI.

entries.goal: Mancala mobile. 6 pits per side + 2 stores at the ends. TAP one of your pits to scoop & sow. End-store rules apply (sow into your own store but skip opponent's). Free-turn rule for landing in your store; capture rule for landing in your empty pit opposite a non-empty enemy pit. Vs-AI heuristic player. Score = stones in your store. Portrait full-bleed (board oriented vertically — pits on left/right columns, stores top/bottom). Persist record. Apply mobile-frontend wisdom rules.
entries.notes: Vertical-board reorientation for portrait is the design challenge. Mancala rules are fiddly — tests rule-engine correctness.

entries.goal: Pac-Man mobile. Top-down maze (12×16 cells, fits portrait) with dots, power-pellets, 4 ghosts. SWIPE in 4 directions to set Pac-Man's next-move-direction (queued — moves at next intersection). Eat all dots = level clear. Power-pellet → ghosts edible (10s). Score + lives top-of-screen. Persist best score per level. Portrait full-bleed. Apply mobile-frontend wisdom rules.
entries.notes: Swipe-queue direction (not on-screen joystick) is the mobile innovation. Tests maze pathfinding + ghost AI states + portrait-fit maze geometry.

entries.goal: Breakout/Arkanoid mobile. Bricks at top, paddle at bottom, ball bounces. DRAG horizontally on the bottom 30% of screen to move paddle (relative — finger position offset, not absolute). TAP anywhere = launch ball. Power-ups fall from broken bricks (multi-ball, wider paddle, lasers). 5+ levels. Persist high score. Portrait full-bleed (taller-than-wide = more bricks, longer fall). Apply mobile-frontend wisdom rules. NOTE: breakout IS saturated; mobile portrait + relative drag-paddle is novel.
entries.notes: Relative-position paddle drag (not absolute) is a critical mobile-touch UX choice. Tests setPointerCapture for paddle that can drift off-finger.

entries.goal: Modern Snake mobile. SWIPE in 4 directions to turn (queued at-grid-cell). Snake grows when eating food. Power-foods: golden (slow-mo for 5s), red (multi-grow), blue (ghost-mode pass through walls 3s). Walls wrap or kill (toggle). Endless mode + timed mode. Persist best length per mode. Portrait full-bleed (taller = more snake-length headroom). Apply mobile-frontend wisdom rules. NOTE: snake IS saturated; mobile + power-foods + mode-pick is the novel angle.
entries.notes: Swipe-queue directional input matches Pac-Man entry — pattern-tests are useful. Power-food state-machines are the L3 stretch.

entries.goal: Mini-golf mobile (top-down). 9 holes, each a single screen (no scrolling). Tap-and-DRAG from ball backwards = aim line + power (length of drag). Release = strike. Ball physics with friction + wall bounces. Obstacles: walls, sand (slows), water (death-reset). Scorecard tracks strokes per hole. Persist best per hole. Portrait full-bleed. Apply mobile-frontend wisdom rules.
entries.notes: Drag-back-to-aim is canonical mobile-golf UX. Tests vector-physics + collision + 9-level state.

entries.goal: Pinball web mini-app (portrait one-screen). Two flippers at bottom — TAP LEFT half of screen = left flipper, TAP RIGHT half = right flipper. Ball physics. Bumpers + ramps + slingshots score points. Multi-ball power-up. PLUNGER strip at bottom: drag-down-to-pull-back-and-release for ball launch with adjustable power. Persist high score. Portrait full-bleed. Apply mobile-frontend wisdom rules.
entries.notes: Tap-half-of-screen flippers + drag-plunger replace tilt-nudge. Pure pointer pinball.

entries.goal: Mobile tower defense (different from PvZ — more like Bloons-TD). Curving path through scenic level, enemies walk along it. DRAG towers from a bottom-strip onto the map (any non-path location). 4 tower types (single-target, AoE, slow, money-gen) each with 3 upgrade levels. Money earned per kill. 10 increasing waves of enemies (basic / fast / armored / boss). Persist best wave + currency. Portrait full-bleed. Apply mobile-frontend wisdom rules.
entries.notes: Path-based TD is structurally different from grid-based PvZ — tests path-following enemy AI + drag-place-tower-anywhere + tower-upgrade UI.

entries.goal: MASH-UP: Candy Crush × Wordle. 6×6 grid of letter-tiles (frosted candy aesthetic). DRAG-swap two adjacent tiles to make a 3+ word horizontally OR vertically (must spell a real English word). Words clear + cascade with gravity. 5-letter word = makes a 'wild' wildcard tile. 6-letter = clears entire row. Score-based level progression. Bundled common-words JSON (~3000 entries). Persist best score + level. Portrait full-bleed. Apply mobile-frontend wisdom rules.
entries.notes: MASH-UP RATIONALE: Sweet Cascade (Candy Crush) scored 6/9 mobile tokens + user said 'is great!' Word-puzzle adds intellectual depth. Combines drag-swap mechanic + word-validity engine. Probably the most novel of the bunch.

entries.goal: MASH-UP: Plants vs Zombies × Chess. 8×8 chess board. White = Plants (pawn=Sunflower, rook=Wallnut, bishop=Peashooter, knight=Cherry-bomb, queen=Cattail, king=House). Black = Zombies (mirrored — pawns are basics, etc). Standard chess movement BUT: capture happens via BOTH pieces fighting (HP-based instead of one-shot — peashooter fires till one dies in same turn). King = the House: zombies must reach + survive 1 turn. Drag-piece-to-square mobile UX. Vs-AI minimax depth-3. Persist W-L-D. Portrait full-bleed. Apply mobile-frontend wisdom rules.
entries.notes: MASH-UP RATIONALE: PvZ scored 9/9 mobile tokens (best). Chess strategic depth + PvZ characters is genuinely fresh. HP-based capture is the novel rule-twist that breaks chess monotony.

entries.goal: MASH-UP: 2048 × Flappy Bird. Side-scrolling endless. Bird auto-scrolls right; pipes have NUMBERED gaps (powers of 2: 2, 4, 8, 16). Bird shows current number. TAP to flap up. Pass through a pipe matching your number = number doubles + score. Wrong pipe = collision. Game over on hitting wall/floor/wrong-pipe. Persist best score. Portrait full-bleed. Apply mobile-frontend wisdom rules.
entries.notes: MASH-UP RATIONALE: Flappy ('Solid!') + 2048 — the merge-doubling intellectual hook adds strategy to pure-twitch flappy. Choice of which pipe to aim for IS the gameplay.

entries.goal: MASH-UP: Jetpack Joyride × Bejeweled. Side-scroll endless runner BUT the world is a vertical 6-column gem-cascade. Jetpack character on the left edge auto-scrolls (no horizontal movement). HOLD to fly UP, RELEASE to fall, dodging gem-walls. Touching matching gems collects them — collect 3+ same-color in a row = combo. Periodic 'gem rain' levels are full match-3 puzzle screens (drag-swap to clear). Portrait full-bleed. Persist distance + total gems. Apply mobile-frontend wisdom rules.
entries.notes: MASH-UP RATIONALE: JetPack ('promising') + Bejeweled — adds collection-strategy to twitch-runner. Periodic puzzle-screens break up pure-physics flow.

entries.goal: MASH-UP: Tetris × Snake. Standard tetris well (10×20). Tetrominos fall. CONTROL = a 'snake' that grows along the bottom — it can absorb dropped blocks into its body (instead of stacking) by slithering under them. Eating blocks = score; stacking blocks = lose like normal tetris. Snake DIES if rammed by stacked blocks. Persist best score. Portrait full-bleed. Apply mobile-frontend wisdom rules. NOTE: tetris IS saturated but the snake-absorbs-blocks twist is genuinely novel.
entries.notes: MASH-UP RATIONALE: Snake + Tetris — both 4-direction-input games, both grid-based. Snake-absorbs-tetrominos is fresh — turns enemy (falling block) into food.

entries.goal: MASH-UP: Pomodoro × Idle RPG. Standard pomodoro (25min work, 5min break). Each completed work session = your character ATTACKS a fantasy monster (boss has HP scaled to expected hours). Each break = your character HEALS or shops. Defeat monster = level-up + new ability + harder monster. Stats: HP, ATK, gold. Inventory persists. Portrait full-bleed. Apply mobile-frontend wisdom rules.
entries.notes: MASH-UP RATIONALE: Pomodoro ('Solid!') + idle-RPG — gamifying work sessions with persistent character progression is a famous productivity hack (Habitica, Forest). Mobile fits — phone is where focus-trackers live.

entries.goal: MASH-UP: Doodle Jump × 2048 (web). Vertical endless platformer — character auto-bounces. Platforms have NUMBERS (2, 4, 8, 16, 32...). When you land on a platform with the same number as your character → both double (you absorb its number). Land on different → just bounce. DRAG horizontally on the bottom 30% of screen to steer (relative offset). Goal: highest single-number reached + total height. Power-ups: number-doublers, halvers. Persist best. Portrait full-bleed. Apply mobile-frontend wisdom rules.
entries.notes: Bottom-drag steering replaces tilt. Bouncing-merge game.

entries.goal: MASH-UP: Tip Calculator × Fitness Tracker. Restaurant bill calculator that ALSO computes calorie-burn-equivalent. Enter bill amount + cuisine type (8 cuisines mapped to avg cal-density). Calc tip + per-person split AS USUAL, but also show: 'This meal = X minutes of running / Y minutes of walking / Z burpees'. Saves last 30 meals to localStorage with timestamp + cal estimate. Portrait full-bleed. Apply mobile-frontend wisdom rules.
entries.notes: MASH-UP RATIONALE: Tip-calc (4/9 mobile-tokens) + health-awareness. Goes from 'utility' to 'utility with surprising value-add.' Cuisine→cal-density mapping is teaching-by-doing for users.

entries.goal: MASH-UP: Temple Run × Typing Test. Runner auto-runs forward in pseudo-3D corridor. Obstacles ahead show WORDS — type the word (mobile soft-keyboard at bottom) before reaching obstacle to vault over it. Long words = bigger obstacles. Mistype = collision. Coins from successful types. Persist WPM + best distance. Portrait full-bleed (full-screen kbd takes bottom 40%). Apply mobile-frontend wisdom rules.
entries.notes: MASH-UP RATIONALE: Temple Run (new) + typing-test (saturated). Endless-runner gives stakes to typing speed — the 'why' that pure typing tests lack.

entries.goal: MASH-UP: Bejeweled × Piano Tiles. Vertical 4-column piano grid scrolling DOWN with colored gem-tiles instead of black tiles. Tap each tile when in strike zone. Tap 3+ same-color gems consecutively = MELODY combo (extra score + WebAudio plays an arpeggio). Tap-and-hold a gem = 'sustain' note. Mistakes break combo. Increasing tempo. Persist best score per song. Portrait full-bleed. Apply mobile-frontend wisdom rules.
entries.notes: MASH-UP RATIONALE: Bejeweled-color-matching + Piano Tiles rhythm-tapping. Color-combos create melodies — every successful play sounds musically pleasant. Tests WebAudio note scheduling + combo state machine.

entries.goal: Atari-2600-Boxing-style overhead boxing. Mobile-portrait 360×600 ring rendered on canvas. Two stick-figure boxers — player (left, blue) vs CPU (right, red). Player controls: TWO touch zones — left half = move pad (drag to pan boxer in 8 directions), right half = jab (tap = quick punch, hold = power punch wind-up + release to throw). CPU uses a state-machine AI (approach if range > 80px, jab if range 40-80px, dodge if player is wound up > 300ms). Each clean hit deducts 1 from the opponent's 16-hit score; first to land 16 wins. 90-second round timer — at zero, higher score wins. Power punch = 3 hits but 600ms wind-up window where player is exposed to a counter. Score boxes top corners, big 'ROUND OVER' banner on win. Game state tracked via requestAnimationFrame loop. Touch-only — no keyboard. Persist best-round-time + win-streak to localStorage. Apply mobile-frontend wisdom rules (mob-001 through mob-012).
entries.notes: First boxing entry. Tests two-zone touch input pattern (left=move, right=action) — a common mobile fighting layout. CPU state-machine on a tight rAF loop is the L2 challenge. User-spec'd Atari Boxing reference.

entries.goal: Touch-football play-caller. Mobile-portrait 360×640 top-down 30-yard field on canvas. Before each down, player taps 1 of 4 plays from a bottom strip: Run, Short Pass, Long Pass, Punt. CPU defense simultaneously rolls a counter-card from {Stop-Run, Pass-D, Blitz, Prevent}. Resolution animation runs 3-5s showing the play unfold (RB carrying / WR routes / punt arc). Yardage is a rock-paper-scissors matchup matrix: Run beats Pass-D (+5..+12), Long Pass beats Stop-Run (+15..+40), Short Pass safe vs all (+3..+8), Blitz crushes Pass plays (-3..+1) but loses to Run (+8). 4 downs to make 10 yards (1st down) → continue drive to score TD = 7pts. Punt resets field position. Game = 4 quarters (4 drives each) vs CPU. Score ticker top, down-and-distance HUD bottom. Touch-only — no keyboard. Persist best-drive-yards + total-TDs + W/L to localStorage. Apply mobile-frontend wisdom rules (mob-001 through mob-012).
entries.notes: First American-football entry. Card-vs-card matchup matrix is the strategy core; animation is illustrative only. No DOM-form-controls; all canvas + tap zones.

entries.goal: Penalty-kick mind-game. Mobile-portrait 360×640. Top half: goal frame divided into 5 zones (top-L, top-R, mid-L, mid-R, ground-center), keeper as an animated diamond patrolling between zones. Bottom: ball icon. SHOOTING PHASE (5 rounds): drag the ball UPWARD toward a zone — release commits the shot. Keeper's AI is a Bayesian frequency tracker: maintains a running tally of which zones the player has shot to, weights the LAST 3 shots 3× heavier than older ones, dives toward the highest-probability zone. KEEPING PHASE (5 rounds): tap a zone to commit a dive BEFORE the CPU shooter releases (CPU aims via inverse-Bayesian — picks the LOWEST-probability zone in your dive history). Shoot scores if keeper guesses wrong; save = no goal. Final score on bottom HUD. Touch-only — no keyboard. Persist all-time aggregate (shots taken, goals scored, saves made) to localStorage. Apply mobile-frontend wisdom rules (mob-001 through mob-012).
entries.notes: Tests opponent-AI-as-frequency-table on top of touch drag-to-aim. The Bayesian keeper makes this an L3 — LLMs default to coin-flip RNG; the test is whether they implement the running-tally + weighted-recent-bias correctly.

entries.goal: Tiny-Town hex city-builder. Mobile-portrait 360×640. 7×9 hex grid drawn on canvas using offset coordinates (axial conversion: q,r → px,py). Bottom 35%: building palette strip (House 20¤, Shop 30¤, Farm 25¤, Mill 40¤, Road 5¤) + coin balance + turn counter. DRAG a building tile UPWARD onto a hex to place it (cost deducts; placement valid only on empty hexes; pointerdown + pointermove + pointerup with setPointerCapture). Tap 'End Turn' to advance: each Farm produces 2 wheat → if a Mill is hex-adjacent, wheat converts to flour → if a Shop is adjacent to that Mill, flour sells for 5¤ (supply chain rewards 3-tile adjacency chain). Each House needs road-adjacency or it deactivates (greyed out, no income). Per-turn passive income: active Houses pay 2¤ each. Game ends at turn 30 — final score = coin balance + (buildings placed × 10). Touch-only — no keyboard. Persist best-score + last-final-layout (full grid as JSON) to localStorage. Apply mobile-frontend wisdom rules (mob-001 through mob-012).
entries.notes: First hex-grid mobile entry. Tests offset-hex coordinate math + supply-chain adjacency rules — non-trivial because LLMs default to square grids. SimCity territory (user spec) without copying SimCity-the-app. Trap: hex-neighbor offsets differ for even vs odd rows; LLMs forget this and rows 0/2/4 have different neighbor sets than rows 1/3/5.

entries.goal: Pocket-Quest party-grid RPG. Mobile-portrait 360×640. Top row: 3 hero portraits (Warrior 30HP/8ATK, Mage 18HP/12ATK, Cleric 22HP/4ATK + heal). Middle: 8×11 dungeon grid drawn on canvas, fog-of-war reveals around heroes. Bottom: action HUD + 3-slot potion inventory. Player taps a hero to select, then taps an adjacent grid cell to move ONE tile (one tick advances enemies). Tap an adjacent enemy cell to attack: rock-paper-scissors damage matrix (Warrior > Cleric > Mage > Warrior with 1.5× type bonus). Cleric's heal: tap own hero → heal 8HP (3-turn cooldown). XP/HP bars per hero, level-up at 100 XP grants +5HP +1ATK. 5 floors total, each ends at a stairs ('>') tile. Bosses on floors 3 and 5. Loot drops (gold, potions) on enemy kill. Persist current-floor + full party state (HP, XP, level, position, inventory) to localStorage so the game RESUMES mid-run on next launch. Touch-only — no keyboard. Apply mobile-frontend wisdom rules (mob-001 through mob-012).
entries.notes: EverQuest-flavored party-grid RPG (user spec). Distinct from existing roguelikes which are solo + ASCII. Mid-run persistence (resume on next launch) is the L3 hook — LLMs often forget to serialize derived state like fog-of-war reveals.

entries.goal: Cipher Clinic decoder game. Mobile-portrait 360×640. Top: ciphertext displayed in monospace (random English sentence 30-60 chars). Middle: large decoded-text scratch row (taps modify this). Bottom: 26-letter touch keypad — tap a letter in the scratch row, then tap a keypad letter to substitute it across all instances. 6 rounds with rising difficulty: rounds 1-2 = Caesar shift (single-key substitution), rounds 3-4 = Vigenère 3-letter key, rounds 5-6 = Vigenère 5-letter key. 'Hint' button auto-decodes a single random ciphertext letter (limit 3 hints/round). Submit button validates exact-match against the plaintext. Score = 1000 - (seconds elapsed × 5) - (hints used × 100), bonus +500 for no-hint clears. Touch-only — no keyboard. Persist best-time-per-round + total rounds-cleared to localStorage. Apply mobile-frontend wisdom rules (mob-001 through mob-012).
entries.notes: Cipher decoder is absent from catalog. Tap-substitute UI exercises pointer pattern + state-keyed-by-letter-mapping. Vigenère vs Caesar is the algorithmic ladder.

entries.goal: Bookworm-style adjacent-letter chain. Mobile-portrait 360×640. 7×7 grid of letter tiles (weighted random distribution favoring vowels). Touch a tile then drag finger across adjacent tiles (8-direction adjacency, no diagonal-skip) to build a word; on release, validate against a 2000-word embedded dictionary (JS array). Valid → letters disappear, tiles above fall to fill gaps via gravity animation, new tiles spawn at the top. Score = sum(letter values, Scrabble scoring) × length-bonus (3=1.0×, 4=1.2×, 5=1.5×, 6+=2.0×). Every 5 valid words: a BOMB tile spawns (red border, must be cleared in 3 moves or game over). 60-second timer per game; new game button resets. Touch-only — no keyboard. Persist top-3 high scores + longest-word found to localStorage. Apply mobile-frontend wisdom rules (mob-001 through mob-012).
entries.notes: Bookworm Adventures-inspired but original 2000-word list. Tests drag-across-grid path-tracing (different from tap-each which is what the existing Anagram-110 uses). Bomb-tile pressure is the secondary mechanic.

entries.goal: Spades w/ AI bots. Mobile-portrait 360×640. 4-player Spades, you + 3 AI (1 partner across, 2 opponents). Standard 52-card deck dealt 13 each. PHASE 1 — Bidding: bottom HUD shows '0' through '13' chips + 'Nil' chip; tap one to commit (AI bots bid via simple high-card-count heuristic, +1 per Ace, +0.5 per King). PHASE 2 — Trick play (13 tricks): your hand fanned at the bottom (touch a card to play). Must follow suit if able; spades cannot be led until 'broken' (someone trumps off-suit). AI uses void-tracking: remembers each player's empty suits and avoids leading them. PHASE 3 — Score: bid met = bid × 10 + 1/overtrick (sandbag). 10-bag penalty = -100. First team to 500 wins; -200 loses. Touch-only — no keyboard. Persist best-game-margin + W/L tally to localStorage. Apply mobile-frontend wisdom rules (mob-001 through mob-012).
entries.notes: Spades absent from catalog. 3-phase trick game (bid → play → score) is the L2 challenge. Void-tracking AI is the planner test — LLMs often skip it and play purely random suit-following.

entries.goal: Rummy 500. Mobile-portrait 360×640. Race to 500 points across multiple hands. You + 1 AI bot. Each hand: deck face-down, discard pile face-up (top card visible). On your turn: tap deck OR tap discard top to draw — IF taking from discard, you take EVERY card BELOW it back to a card you can immediately meld (Rummy 500 rule). LAY DOWN: tap 'Meld' → select 3+ cards from your hand forming a Set (3+ same rank) or Run (3+ consecutive same-suit). LAY-OFF: tap 'Add' on opponent's existing meld + select 1 of your cards to extend it (only if it legally fits). End turn = tap a card + 'Discard'. Hand ends when one player has 0 cards — melds count + (opponent's leftover cards count -against- them). First to 500 wins. Touch-only — no keyboard. Persist running tally + best-game-margin to localStorage. Apply mobile-frontend wisdom rules (mob-001 through mob-012).
entries.notes: Rummy 500 absent. Lay-off on opponent's existing melds is the non-trivial state mechanic + 'take everything below' discard rule pushes this to L3. Common LLM trap: implementing standard Rummy (no lay-off) and missing the 'take everything below in discard pile' nuance.

entries.goal: 5×5 Mini-Shogi (drop-piece variant). Mobile-portrait 360×640. 5×5 board centered. Each side starts with: King (center back), Rook (right), Bishop (left), Silver (between Rook+King), Pawn (front of King). Standard Shogi piece movement. CAPTURED PIECES go to your hand (drop-strip below the board) and can be DROPPED back onto any empty cell on your turn instead of moving (key Shogi rule). Tap your piece → highlights legal moves with green dots → tap destination. Tap a hand-piece chip → highlights legal drops → tap an empty cell. Win on King-mate (no escape from check). AI uses 3-ply minimax with simple piece-value evaluation (King∞, Rook 5, Bishop 4, Silver 3, Pawn 1). Touch-only — no keyboard. Persist W/L count + last-game-board to localStorage (so user can review the last game). Apply mobile-frontend wisdom rules (mob-001 through mob-012).
entries.notes: Mini-Shogi absent. Drop-piece rule is the unique mechanic — distinguishes from chess/checkers (which are saturated). 3-ply minimax on a 5×5 board is fast; LLMs can implement without alpha-beta.

entries.goal: Reversi / Othello. Mobile-portrait 360×640. 8×8 board, classic black-vs-white discs. Touch any LEGAL cell to place a disc — legal cells are highlighted with light-gray dots BEFORE you tap. Placing flips all opposing discs in straight lines (horizontal, vertical, diagonal) bracketed between your existing pieces and the new placement, with smooth flip animations (CSS transform 0.3s rotateY). AI = greedy 'max-flip' heuristic (NOT minimax — too easy, prevents fast L2 solve): at each AI turn, play the move that flips the most opposing discs RIGHT NOW. Score readout (B:N W:N) updates after each move. Game ends when neither side has a legal move — highest count wins. Touch-only — no keyboard. Persist W/L tally + best-margin to localStorage. Apply mobile-frontend wisdom rules (mob-001 through mob-012).
entries.notes: Reversi/Othello absent from miniApp catalog (note: macOS slot 142 also has an Othello but with GKMinmaxStrategist — deliberate pair to compare planner output for greedy-vs-minmax).

entries.goal: Risk-lite 8-territory dice conquest. Mobile-portrait 360×640. Stylized world map with 8 color-coded territories drawn as polygons on canvas (3 in a north 'continent', 3 in a south 'continent', 2 island-bonus). Player vs 2 AI. PHASE 1 — Reinforce: receive armies = (own territories / 3) + continent-bonus (own all 3 of a continent = +2/turn). Tap own territory N times to deposit armies. PHASE 2 — Attack: tap own territory → tap adjacent enemy territory → dice rolls resolve (attacker rolls min(3, armies) dice, defender min(2, armies); compare highest pair, attacker loses ties). Repeat until won (attacker moves at least 1 army into conquered territory) or stop. PHASE 3 — Fortify: optionally move armies from 1 own territory to 1 adjacent own territory once. Auto-end-turn after fortify. AI uses simple 'attack the weakest neighbor first' heuristic. Win = own all 8 territories. Touch-only — no keyboard. Persist last 3 game outcomes (W/L + turns taken) to localStorage. Apply mobile-frontend wisdom rules (mob-001 through mob-012).
entries.notes: Risk-lite — first turn-based-conquest entry. 3-phase turn structure (reinforce/attack/fortify) is the L3 hook. Bounded to 8 territories so AI evaluation stays tractable.

entries.goal: Skee-Ball flick-aim. Mobile-portrait 360×640 vertical alley canvas. View: ramp at top with target rings (10pt outer, 20, 30, 50, 100pt center) inside a 'mouth' opening; bowling-ball icon at bottom on a launch pad. DRAG-FLICK: pointerdown on ball, drag DOWNWARD-then-UPWARD (swipe-up gesture), the velocity-vector at release determines initial speed + angle. Ball rolls up the ramp under inverted-gravity (rAF physics: friction 0.98/frame, gravity decelerates upward), arcs over the mouth, and lands in a ring (or misses into the gutter = 0pts). 9 balls per game. Score totals + best-9-frame. Touch-only — no keyboard. Persist best-9-frame + total-100-pointers-landed to localStorage. Apply mobile-frontend wisdom rules (mob-001 through mob-012).
entries.notes: Flick-aim with velocity vector at release is the unique input mechanic — tests pointer history tracking (last 3 positions for velocity calc). LLMs often use just pointerup position, missing the velocity component.

entries.goal: Tap-bumper score-chase. Mobile-portrait 360×640 canvas. 5 stationary bumper circles arranged in a pentagram pattern. A ball bounces around the canvas (rAF physics: gravity 0.3, walls reflect). Tap any bumper — if the ball is within 60px of that bumper at tap-time, the bumper 'kicks' the ball (impulse vector away from bumper center, +5pts to score). Each bumper has a 300ms cooldown after a kick (visual: bumper dims). Goal: keep the ball alive (ball drops off bottom = game over) while racking score. 60-second round timer (UI shows remaining time in big font top-right). Touch-only — no flippers, no plunger — pure tap-timing on the 5 bumpers. Persist top-3 scores to localStorage. Apply mobile-frontend wisdom rules (mob-001 through mob-012).
entries.notes: Distinct from existing Pinball-128 (full flippers + plunger). Pure-tap-bumper is a thumb-friendly mobile take. Tests pointer-vs-ball-position distance test + impulse vector math.

entries.goal: 4-lane Beat-Tap rhythm runner. Mobile-portrait 360×640. 4 vertical lanes spanning full height, hit-line drawn at bottom 25%. Notes (colored circles) spawn at the top of random lanes and fall at 4px/frame toward the hit-line. Player taps the lane (any vertical position within the lane's column) when a note crosses the hit-line. Timing windows: Perfect = ±60ms (+100pts, builds combo), Good = ±150ms (+50pts), Miss = note crosses hit-line untapped (combo resets). 90-second song with procedural beat track via Web Audio (sin osc kick at 120 BPM = 2 beats/sec, with snare-hat fills). Note pattern: scripted 180 notes evenly distributed across lanes/time. Combo counter top-center, score top-right. Touch-only — no keyboard. Persist best-score + max-combo to localStorage. Apply mobile-frontend wisdom rules (mob-001 through mob-012). Distinct from Piano-Tiles-097 because timing windows are tight and lanes scroll continuously rather than tile-tap-when-arrived.
entries.notes: First rhythm-game entry with tight timing windows. Tests Audio-time alignment (performance.now() vs AudioContext.currentTime) — common LLM trap is using rAF time which drifts from audio clock.

entries.goal: Card-Crawl 4×3 dungeon-as-cards. Mobile-portrait 360×640. Game state = 4-column × 3-row grid of cards face-up at all times (12 cards visible). Cards: Monster (HP value, attacks back when taken), Weapon (durability, kills 1 monster equal-or-lower HP), Shield (blocks N damage, single-use), Potion (+5HP). Player HP shown top (start 30HP). Player taps a card to TAKE it (resolve effect). Rule: each TURN you must take exactly 3 of the 4 cards in the TOP row before that row refreshes from a 30-card deck (drawn 4 at a time as cards are taken). 'Skip card' allowed once per row — the skipped card stays for next row's refresh. Game ends when deck exhausted (clear) or HP ≤ 0 (loss). Score = HP remaining × 10 + monsters killed. Touch-only — no keyboard. Persist deck-cleared count + best-HP-remaining to localStorage. Apply mobile-frontend wisdom rules (mob-001 through mob-012).
entries.notes: Card-Crawl-inspired (Arnold Rauers' game) but original deck. Take-3-of-4 rule + skip-card pressure + weapon-durability vs monster-HP arithmetic = the L3 strategy puzzle.

entries.goal: Galaxian-style swooping shmup. Mobile-portrait 360×640 canvas. Player turret pinned to bottom-center, drag-pad on bottom 30% of screen scrubs turret horizontally. Tap-anywhere-on-top-70% fires a single bullet upward. Aliens enter from off-screen top in waves: each wave = 16 aliens that fly in along procedurally-curved arc paths (parametric Bezier per alien), then settle into a 4-row × 4-col stationary formation in the upper third. ATTACK PHASE: every 1.5s a random formation alien BREAKS FORMATION, swoops down at the player on a sine-wave path, fires a bullet, then either kamikazes (collides = -1 life) or returns to formation. 3 lives, 3 increasing waves (more aliens, faster swoops). Score top-left, lives top-right. Touch-only — no keyboard. Persist top-3 scores + max-wave-cleared to localStorage. Apply mobile-frontend wisdom rules (mob-001 through mob-012).
entries.notes: Galaxian's swoop-from-formation AI is what distinguishes it from existing arcade-shooters (Galaga is in catalog at 079 but as macOS-only Comet-Sweeper-style; this is the mobile touch flavor with Bézier swoop paths).

entries.goal: Q*bert isometric pyramid hopper. Mobile-portrait 360×640. Isometric pyramid of 28 cubes (7-row triangle: row 1 = 1 cube, row 2 = 2, ..., row 7 = 7) drawn with isometric projection on canvas. Player character (Q*bert sprite, simple round body) starts on top cube. Bottom of screen: 4 quadrant tap-zones (NE / NW / SE / SW) shown as large arrow buttons. Tap a quadrant → character hops diagonally to the adjacent cube in that direction; cube color toggles (gray → yellow on first hop, that's a 'flipped' cube). Goal: flip ALL 28 cubes. Snake enemy spawns from top after 5s, traverses pyramid downward at 1 hop/sec; collision with player = lose 1 life (3 lives total). Falling off the pyramid edge = lose 1 life. Game ends when all flipped (advance level, +1 snake) or out of lives. Touch-only — no keyboard. Persist best-level + cubes-flipped-total to localStorage. Apply mobile-frontend wisdom rules (mob-001 through mob-012).
entries.notes: Isometric projection drawing + diagonal-hop-only movement is the L2 spec. Quadrant-tap-zones is a cleaner mobile idiom than virtual D-pad.

entries.goal: Kaboom! falling-bombs bucket-paddle. Mobile-portrait 360×640 canvas. Top: bomber sprite walks left-to-right-and-back, dropping bombs at irregular intervals (waves get faster + drop-rate higher). Bottom: stack of 3 buckets (cyan rectangles, vertically stacked) controlled together by player drag-tracking horizontally. DRAG anywhere on screen → bucket-stack X-position follows finger (clamped to canvas bounds). Bombs fall under gravity (3px/frame initial, +0.05/frame). Bomb hits TOP bucket → caught (+10pts). Bomb hits floor — explosion animation, lose the BOTTOM bucket (stack drops one) + reset wave. All buckets gone = game over. 8 increasing waves (each completion = +1 bucket back, refill to 3 max). Touch-only — no keyboard. Persist top-3 scores + max-wave-cleared to localStorage. Apply mobile-frontend wisdom rules (mob-001 through mob-012).
entries.notes: Drag-track via setPointerCapture is the touch-correct pattern — LLMs sometimes use mousemove without capture and lose tracking when finger leaves the bucket area.

entries.goal: 501-Down darts w/ flick-aim. Mobile-portrait 360×640. Top half: dartboard drawn on canvas with concentric rings (bullseye 50, bull 25, treble ring, double ring, plus the standard 20-sector wedge layout). Bottom half: drag-flick zone (large dark area). DRAG within the flick zone: drag distance = power, drag angle = aim trajectory; pointerup = release — dart flies via parabolic arc from bottom toward dartboard. Where it lands determines score. Each player starts at 501; subtract score each throw. Must finish on a DOUBLE (or bullseye). 3 darts per turn, alternating with 1 AI bot (AI uses random-with-aim-bias: aims for treble-20, fuzzes by ±15°). Bust on overshoot (turn ends with no score). Persist best-game + W/L tally to localStorage. Touch-only — no keyboard. Apply mobile-frontend wisdom rules (mob-001 through mob-012).
entries.notes: Dartboard sector-detection is the L2 spec — takes math (cartesian → polar → sector index). LLMs sometimes hardcode 20 if-blocks; the test is whether they reach for atan2 + modular arithmetic.

entries.goal: Archery target-bow with wind. Mobile-portrait 360×640. Top half: archery target (concentric rings 10/9/8/.../1 from center) at 'distance', wind-arrow widget top-right (random per shot, e.g. '5 mph ↗' = 5px right + 5px up nudge during flight). Bottom half: bow icon centered, drawstring drawn from bow to current finger position. DRAG-PULL-RELEASE: pointerdown on bow → drag DOWN to set tension (max 60pt downward = full draw); release angle (vector from bow center to release point) determines arrow direction. Arrow flies via projectile motion (gravity + wind nudge mid-flight) and lands on target. Score = ring (1-10) it lands in, miss = 0. 6 arrows / 3 rounds = 18 arrows total. Persist best total + best-single-shot. Touch-only — no keyboard. Apply mobile-frontend wisdom rules (mob-001 through mob-012).
entries.notes: Drag-pull-release with wind compensation — tests vector math + animated mid-flight perturbation. Distinct from Skee-Ball-152 (which has friction-decel, no projectile gravity).

entries.goal: Pickleball overhead-court 1v1. Mobile-portrait 360×640 canvas — court drawn from above (player on bottom half, AI on top half, net horizontal across middle, kitchen no-volley zones marked). Ball bounces back and forth between players; rallies physics-resolve via rAF (gravity-less 2D bounce off court edges, ball decel 0.99/frame). PLAYER STROKE: when ball is on your half, drag from current ball position (auto-attached to your finger if within 80px) toward intended landing spot — pointerup commits stroke. Drag DISTANCE = depth of return (longer drag = harder hit); drag ANGLE = lateral direction. Hold-time before release = topspin (more spin = more bounce-curve). AI mirrors player on top (heuristic: aim away from player's last position). 11-point game, must win by 2. Each rally physics-resolves on canvas. Touch-only — no keyboard. Persist W/L + best-rally-length (longest rally count) to localStorage. Apply mobile-frontend wisdom rules (mob-001 through mob-012).
entries.notes: First overhead-court touch sport. Drag-from-ball-toward-target is the unique stroke idiom (cleaner than tap-to-hit, more expressive than swipe). Hold-time-as-spin is the bonus mechanic — LLMs often miss this and just use distance for power.

entries.goal: Pole Position pseudo-3D corner-cutter. Mobile-portrait 360×640 canvas. Pseudo-3D road rendered with trapezoidal canvas strips that recede toward a vanishing point (like an FPS depth illusion). 6 strips per frame, each strip's left/right X offset shifts according to current 'curvature' value (animated to procedurally curve the road left/right). Player car drawn at bottom-center. DRAG-PAD on bottom 30% = steer (drag-X delta updates car's lateral position). Throttle is auto-on (constant forward speed). Oncoming-traffic cars spawn at vanishing point, grow as they approach — collision (player-car bounding-box overlaps oncoming-car bounding-box on bottom row) = -3sec time penalty + crash animation (car spins). 60-second time-trial; reach finish line (after 4000 'meters' simulated road) = bonus points. Distance HUD top + lap timer top-right. Touch-only — no keyboard. Persist top-3 lap times to localStorage. Apply mobile-frontend wisdom rules (mob-001 through mob-012).
entries.notes: Pseudo-3D depth-projection trapezoids is the unique L2 rendering trick. LLMs default to side-scrollers; the test is whether they implement perspective vanishing-point math + curvature-by-strip-offset.

entries.goal: Spy Hunter top-down road-shooter. Mobile-portrait 360×640 canvas. Top-down 4-lane road scrolls upward beneath a stationary player car at bottom-center. SWIPE LEFT / SWIPE RIGHT (horizontal touch gesture, threshold 30px) = lane-swap (1 lane). TAP UPPER HALF = fire bullet straight forward. Enemy cars approach from top (different colors): red = aggressive (rams sideways), gray = neutral (just blocks lane), yellow = weapon-van (catch up + drive into the back to refill ammo from 0 to 10). Player ammo HUD top-left, score top-right, lives bottom-right (3 lives). Periodic semi-truck spawn = weapon-van event. Crash with red car or run-into-gray-car = -1 life. Touch-only — no keyboard. Persist top-3 scores to localStorage. Apply mobile-frontend wisdom rules (mob-001 through mob-012).
entries.notes: Swipe-to-lane-swap is the touch idiom — distinct from continuous-drag steering. Weapon-van refill mechanic is the secondary loop. LLMs sometimes implement swipe as continuous follow-finger; the test is whether they detect a discrete swipe gesture (deltaX > threshold within Nms).

entries.goal: Slalom course w/ gate-snake drag-carving. Mobile-portrait 360×640 canvas. View: skier sprite centered, mountain scrolls UPWARD (creates illusion of downhill speed). Slalom GATES spawn at random horizontal positions and scroll up: each gate = a left-marker (red flag) + right-marker (blue flag) ~80px apart. Player must drag-carve the skier between the markers. DRAG anywhere on screen → skier X-position follows finger horizontally (clamped to slope edges). Pass between markers = gate cleared (+1 to gate counter). Miss a gate (skier goes outside the marker pair when it crosses the skier's Y line) = +1.0sec time penalty + red flash. 60-gate course; scoring = total elapsed time (lower is better). Speed increases gradually (every 10 gates). Touch-only — no keyboard. Persist top-3 times + total-perfect-runs (zero penalties) to localStorage. Apply mobile-frontend wisdom rules (mob-001 through mob-012).
entries.notes: Reverse-scroll (mountain scrolls upward) creates downhill illusion — LLMs default to player moving down a static map. Drag-track skier between gate markers tests precise drag-following + Y-crossing collision detection.

entries.goal: Donkey Kong-style climbing platformer. Mobile-portrait 360x640 canvas. 4 sloped girders stacked vertically, ladders connecting them. Player (Mario-like jumper) starts bottom-left, must climb to the top where Donkey Kong (large gorilla sprite) periodically rolls barrels down. Barrels travel along girders and fall to the next girder at the slope-end (or randomly). On-screen virtual D-pad on bottom 30% of screen: LEFT/RIGHT walk, UP/DOWN climb ladder when on one, JUMP button (top-right) to hop over barrels. Hit by barrel = lose life (3 lives). Reach top = next level (faster barrels). Score: +100 per barrel jumped, +500 per level cleared. Hammer power-up that destroys barrels for 5s. Score + lives top-of-screen. Portrait full-bleed. Apply mobile-frontend wisdom rules.
entries.notes: Tests vertical-platformer physics + ladder-climbing state + barrel rolling AI + slope-following on canvas. Different family from horizontal side-scroll platformers (Mario) — emphasis is on vertical traversal + dodging projectiles.

entries.goal: Mario-style side-scrolling platformer. Mobile-portrait 360x640 canvas. World scrolls LEFT as player runs RIGHT. Tile-based level (32px tiles) with ground, floating platforms (?-blocks + brick blocks), pipes (some warp, most decorative), a goal flag at the end. Goombas walk back-and-forth on platforms (1 tile bounding box, reverse on edge). Stomping a Goomba (land on top) = kill + small bounce; running into a Goomba = lose. Coins float above platforms (collect on touch). Question-blocks reveal a coin or a mushroom (tap from below). Mushroom = grow Big Mario (1 hit absorb). On-screen virtual D-pad on bottom 30%: LEFT/RIGHT run, JUMP button (top-right), variable-height jump (longer hold = higher). Reach goal flag = level cleared, advance to level 2 (3 levels total, hand-designed). Lives + coins + level top-of-screen. Persist best level reached. Portrait full-bleed. Apply mobile-frontend wisdom rules.
entries.notes: Side-scrolling platformer family — different from Donkey Kong's vertical climb. Tests scrolling-camera math + tile-based collision + variable-height jump physics + simple AI (Goomba reverse-on-edge) + power-up state. Hand-designed levels (not procedural) so we test level-data-as-content.

entries.goal: OutRun-style pseudo-3D arcade racer. Mobile-portrait 360x640 canvas. View: third-person behind player car, road recedes to horizon (pseudo-3D rendered with trapezoidal canvas strips, NOT WebGL). Road curves left and right with smooth interpolation; alternating asphalt + shoulder stripes scroll past for speed sensation. Sky changes color through 5 tracks (dawn -> day -> dusk -> sunset -> night). Player car pinned to bottom-center; left/right tilt-style steering via DRAG on bottom 30% of screen (relative drag, not absolute). Throttle/brake = on-screen pedals top-corners. Other cars (3-5 visible) drive in same direction at varying speeds; collision = spin-out + 2s control loss + speed drops. Each track is a fixed 60s timer; reach the goal post before timer expires = unlock next track + bonus 30s carries over. Speed shown in km/h, time + score top-of-screen. Persist best time per track. Portrait full-bleed. Apply mobile-frontend wisdom rules.
entries.notes: Different from Pole Position (also pseudo-3D but corner-cutting focused) — OutRun emphasis is sky-changing checkpoint racing + traffic dodging, not corner apex precision. Tests pseudo-3D road math (z-depth -> screen-y, road-curve interpolation) + checkpoint-timer pacing + traffic AI.

entries.goal: Bomberman-style top-down maze bomber. Mobile-portrait 360x640 canvas. 11x9 tile grid (32px tiles, fits portrait). Indestructible stone walls (in a fixed checkerboard pattern), destructible brick walls (random), open floor. Player at top-left. On-screen virtual D-pad bottom-30%: 4-direction tile-walk. PLACE BOMB button (top-right). Bombs explode after 2.5s in a +-pattern, 1-tile radius. Explosions destroy bricks + kill enemies + kill player (3 lives). 3 wandering enemies that walk randomly + reverse on wall. Powerups under bricks: extra-bomb (max 3 active), longer-flame (1->2->3 tile radius), extra-life. Clear all enemies = next level (more enemies, faster). Score + lives + level top-of-screen. Persist best score per level. Portrait full-bleed. Apply mobile-frontend wisdom rules.
entries.notes: Tests grid-locked movement + bomb timer state + explosion BFS + powerup-spawn-on-destroy. Distinct from other arcade games — emphasis on planning (place bomb, retreat, time the explosion).

entries.goal: Lunar Lander mobile. Mobile-portrait 360x640 canvas. Side-view: starry black sky, jagged terrain at bottom rendered as line segments with one or two narrow flat 'landing pads' (worth more if smaller). Lander sprite (small triangular ship) starts top with downward velocity + sideways drift. Gravity pulls down constantly. Two on-screen buttons bottom: THRUST (hold to fire main engine, consumes fuel) on right, TILT-LEFT/TILT-RIGHT on left (rotates lander 5 degrees per tap, vector thrust applies in the lander's facing direction). HUD: fuel bar, altitude, vertical-velocity, horizontal-velocity, rotation. Land on a pad with vy<2, vx<1, rotation within +-5deg = WIN (score = remaining fuel x pad-multiplier). Crash anywhere else = explosion + retry. 3 procedurally-generated levels (steeper terrain, smaller pads, less fuel). Persist best score. Portrait full-bleed. Apply mobile-frontend wisdom rules.
entries.notes: Tests vector physics (gravity + thrust + rotation) + procedural terrain generation + tight landing tolerance. Different family from horizontal-scroll games — vertical descent emphasis.

entries.goal: Marble Madness-style isometric ball-roll. Mobile-portrait 360x640 canvas. Isometric view of a 12x12 tile board with elevation: ramps, gaps (instant death), narrow paths, conveyor strips that push the ball, ice tiles (low friction). Ball rolls based on TILT physics: DRAG anywhere on screen = tilt direction, distance from pointer = tilt angle. Ball gains momentum and slides predictably. Reach the goal tile (large green X) before the 60s timer = win + bonus seconds carry to next level. 4 hand-designed levels increasing in elevation complexity. Score = remaining time + 100 per level. Falling off = -10s penalty, respawn at last checkpoint. Persist best time per level. Portrait full-bleed. Apply mobile-frontend wisdom rules.
entries.notes: Tests iso projection (cube-of-tiles + elevation) + tilt-relative-momentum physics + checkpoint state. Distinct from top-down 4-direction games — emphasis on smooth analog motion + elevation 'feel'.

entries.goal: Excitebike-style side-scrolling motocross. Mobile-portrait 360x640 canvas (portrait, but the 'view' is rotated — landscape course as a horizontal strip in the middle 50% of screen). Bike auto-scrolls left-to-right; world scrolls past. Course has ramps (jump physics), bumps (small hops), oil slicks (wipeout), and rival bikes weaving in the same lanes (bump them to slow, get bumped to wipeout). On-screen: WHEELIE button (top-right) to lift front wheel — necessary to land jumps cleanly (over-rotate or under = wipeout); ACCEL/BRAKE pedals on bottom-corners. 3 lanes (UP/DOWN swipe to change). Engine temperature gauge — hold accel too long = overheat = forced slowdown. 4 increasing courses. Race against a rival ghost (last-best-time) for best-effect; finish = next course. Persist best time per course. Apply mobile-frontend wisdom rules.
entries.notes: Tests jump-arc physics + lane-change snapping + temperature management + ghost-replay state. Different family from OutRun (3rd-person racer) — side-view motocross with hop-over-ramps focus.

entries.goal: Crystal Caves-style descent miner. Mobile-portrait 360x640 canvas. Top-down 16x24 grid of soil cells. Player digs straight down/sideways by walking; gravity pulls boulders down when their support is removed. Crystals scattered through the soil — collect by walking onto them (+10 points each). Boulders can crush you if they fall on top (lose life). On-screen virtual D-pad bottom-30%: 4-direction tile-walk-and-dig. Soil regenerates? — NO; once dug, stays dug. Reach the bottom row = level complete + bonus per crystal. 5 hand-designed levels (more boulders, fewer safe paths). HUD: crystals collected, lives, level. Persist best level reached. Apply mobile-frontend wisdom rules.
entries.notes: Tests grid-cell mutation + falling-boulder cascade physics + hand-designed level data. Distinct from Tetris (puzzle) and from Bomberman (no enemies). Mining + gravity puzzle hybrid.

entries.goal: Lemmings-mini puzzle. Mobile-portrait 360x640 canvas. Side-view 2D level (32-tile-wide, ~10-tall, hand-drawn pixel art). Lemmings spawn from a TRAPDOOR top-left at intervals (1 every 2s); they walk right, fall when reaching a cliff, reverse on a wall. Goal: get N lemmings to the EXIT door (bottom-right), avoiding hazards (cliffs to deep pits, fire, water). Bottom toolbar: 5 ability buttons — BLOCKER (lemming stops + becomes a blocking tile), DIGGER (digs straight down), BUILDER (places 6 step-up tiles diagonally), CLIMBER (one lemming climbs walls), FLOATER (one lemming opens parachute). TAP an ability, then TAP a lemming to apply. 3 levels with limited ability counts (e.g. 2 builders + 1 digger + 1 blocker). Save 50%+ of lemmings to advance. Persist best save-rate per level. Apply mobile-frontend wisdom rules.
entries.notes: Tests crowd-AI (multiple lemmings simultaneously walking + reversing + ability-applying) + hand-designed levels + ability-budget puzzle constraint. High-complexity AI per-entity bookkeeping.

entries.goal: Asteroids vector-graphics shooter. Mobile-portrait 360x640 canvas. Single-screen wraparound (ship + asteroids that exit a side reappear on the opposite side). Triangle SHIP rotates LEFT/RIGHT via two on-screen buttons (bottom-30%); THRUST button on bottom-right (hold for forward thrust + ship-facing momentum); FIRE button (top-right) shoots a single bullet that travels 2 seconds before disappearing. 4 large asteroids drifting at start; shooting one splits it into 2 medium asteroids; mediums into 2 small; smalls disappear. Hit by asteroid = lose ship (3 ships total). Clear all asteroids = next level (more asteroids, faster). Score: +20 large, +50 medium, +100 small. Occasional UFO that drifts across firing back at the player (+200 if killed). Persist best score. Apply mobile-frontend wisdom rules. Vector-style line art (white lines on black).
entries.notes: Already have mini-l3-games-asteroids-077 (desktop variant) but no mobile-portrait one. Touch-friendly buttons + screen-wrap + asteroid-split-cascade.

entries.goal: Snake classic mobile. Mobile-portrait 360x640. Top-down grid (12x18 cells fits portrait). Snake starts as 3-segment line at center, moving right at 4 cells/sec. SWIPE in 4 directions to set next-direction (queued at next cell-tick — can't reverse onto self). Apple appears at random empty cell — eating it grows snake +1 segment + speeds up by 2%. Hit wall = game over. Hit self = game over. Score = segments x 10. Persist best score. Bonus golden apple every 10 apples (+50 score, doesn't grow). Pause button top-right. Apply mobile-frontend wisdom rules. NOTE: there is already a desktop snake — make this distinctly MOBILE-PORTRAIT-FIRST with SWIPE controls + responsive grid sizing.
entries.notes: Mobile-first Snake. Catches: most snake builds get desktop arrow-key controls; this one needs SWIPE + portrait grid. Should be quick (~5-8min L1 fast-path).

entries.goal: Zaxxon-style isometric scrolling shmup. Mobile-portrait 360x640 canvas. Isometric (45-degree axonometric) projection. World auto-scrolls diagonally (toward upper-right). Player ship sprite controlled by DRAG in bottom-30%: drag relative-x moves L/R, drag relative-y adjusts altitude (higher = ship rendered with shadow farther from ship body). Walls + gun-emplacements on the ground + flying enemies in the air. ALTITUDE matters: ground gun-emplacements only hit you if you're low; flying enemies only collide if your altitude matches theirs. FIRE button top-right shoots straight ahead. 3 hand-designed wave/sectors. Fuel meter — collect fuel-bursts to keep flying. Persist best score. Apply mobile-frontend wisdom rules.
entries.notes: Tests ISO projection (axonometric, NOT perspective) + altitude as a third axis driving collision rules + diagonal world scroll. Real test of 'fake 3D' techniques on canvas.

entries.goal: Tron Light Cycles 1v1. Mobile-portrait 360x640 canvas. Top-down arena (vertical bias to fit portrait — taller than wide). Two cycles: player (cyan) + AI (orange). Both move forward continuously at constant speed, leaving a colored trail behind. Trails are walls. Crashing into a wall (any trail or arena edge) = lose. Player turns by SWIPING LEFT or RIGHT on the bottom-30% (relative to current direction — swipe-left = turn 90deg left, swipe-right = turn 90deg right). AI uses simple lookahead (avoids walls 5 cells ahead, prefers center, occasionally aggressive cut-off). Best-of-5 rounds. Score = rounds won. Persist best round-streak. Apply mobile-frontend wisdom rules. Neon glow visual.
entries.notes: Tests trail-as-wall collision + simple AI heuristic + swipe-direction-relative-to-heading control. Distinct from Snake — both cycles are walls, plus 1v1 AI dynamic.
